content_html:
INF10025Data Management and Analytics
Week 03 - Part 2
Update & Delete queries

Update Query
An Update Query allows you to change the value of a field(s) for many records at one time.
Example: We can set all employees HoursWorkedLastYear to zero

Update Query
When the query is run, Access gives a warning about impeding changes to the table.
(These warnings can be suppressed via Access Settings)
Once you say Yes, the records are changed.

Update Query
Warning:
There is no way to undo an update query. Ctrl Z is useless
Be careful before you execute an Update Query.
E.g. It is possible to update every row in a 10 million row table.If you specify a criteria incorrectly and do not test you query first, this could have catastrophic results.
It is strongly suggested that you take backups of your data – especially before executing such a query.

Update Query
This example shows how the current Hourly Rate value can be increased by $2.50 for each of the employees. The calculation includes the name of the field.

Delete Query
A Delete Query is similar to an Update Query.
Rows are permanently deleted from a table.
These is no undo facility.
Backups of database tables are crucial.

CrossTab Query
A Cross Tab Query is similar to a Pivot Table in Excel.
A crosstab query performs an aggregation (sum, count…) & groups the results by two sets of values - one set of values on the side of the datasheet and the other set of values across the top.

SQL
Almost all Relational Databases use SQL (structured query language) to manipulate objects and data within the database.
This is hidden from you when you use Access because
Access uses a Graphical User Interface.
Many non-computer savvy people would have trouble understanding SQL
However, each time you Create a Table or Design a Query, Access is generating SQL behind the scenes.
You can view the SQL that is being generated by Access for a number of actions you perform.
(We will be writing some SQL without help from Access in future weeks)

SQL
This is an example of a simple query design screen
The bottom right corner of the screen has an SQL icon.
Click the icon to reveal the SQL statement.
